system.description:
Discipline: Waijia
Skill: Leg Strike or Arm Strike against TN 1 per 10 feet fallen
Type: Counter
Qi: 1

When you hit the ground (or another similar surface) after a fall, it is the ground that loses. You can use this when you are thrown to the ground as part of an Attack or when you fall from heights. On a Success, at the end of your fall, you literally strike the ground, transferring the energy of your fall harmlessly out of yourself. The area that you impact is usually cracked or otherwise damaged by the force of the impact. On a Total Success, you are invigorated by the impact and gain a +1d10 bonus to your next action. On a Failure, you take falling Damage as usual (but see Cathartic below). Cathartic: You automatically are considered to have succeeded on your Leg Strike or Arm Strike roll. This can be done even if you’ve already failed your non-Cathartic Skill roll. Because this is the result of a failed roll, using this skill in this manner always costs 4 Imbalance Points.
system.source: Wandering Heroes of Ogre Gate, Chapter 3: Kung Fu Techniques
system.activationSkill: Leg Strike or Arm Strike against TN 1 per 10 feet fallen
system.catharticEffect: You automatically are considered to have succeeded on your Leg Strike or Arm Strike roll. This can be done even if you’ve already failed your non-Cathartic Skill roll. Because this is the result of a failed roll, using this skill in this manner always costs 4 Imbalance Points.
